body:
## Summary
- Add `project_id` / `project_url` dispatch inputs and `AGENT_PROJECT_MANAGEMENT_PROJECT_ID` / `AGENT_PROJECT_MANAGEMENT_PROJECT_URL` variable support.
- Pass the resolved Project target into the project-manager prompt as experimental context only.
- Update docs and envelope coverage for no-Project summary/dry-run behavior and legacy label opt-in boundaries.

## Verification
- `npm --prefix .agent run build`
- `node --test .agent/dist/__tests__/envelope.test.js .agent/dist/__tests__/apply-project-management-labels-cli.test.js`
- YAML parse check for `.github/workflows/agent-project-manager.yml`
- `git diff --check`

Closes #142

## Rubrics Review

| Total Score | Verdict | Rubrics Scored |
| -- | -- | -- |
| 100 | PASS | 8 |

| Dimension | Rubric | Result | Score | Evidence |
| -- | -- | -- | -- | -- |
| coding_workflow / generic | Prefer explicit structured inputs | pass | 7/7 | Adds separate `project_id` and `project_url` workflow inputs plus separate `AGENT_PROJECT_MANAGEMENT_PROJECT_ID` / `AGENT_PROJECT_MANAGEMENT_PROJECT_URL` config paths in [`agent-project-manager.yml`](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/blob/8c3fc34a3d704d245385c71942c1732db9fee0b1/.github/workflows/agent-project-manager.yml). |
| coding_workflow / generic | Validate preview contracts | pass | 7/7 | The config step validates Project ID whitespace and Project URL shape before the agent runs, while docs and prompt preserve no-Project summary/dry-run behavior. |
| coding_workflow / generic | Keep docs in sync | pass | 7/7 | Updates architecture and configuration docs alongside workflow/prompt changes: [`project-planning.md`](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/blob/8c3fc34a3d704d245385c71942c1732db9fee0b1/.agent/docs/architecture/project-planning.md), [`supported-workflows.md`](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/blob/8c3fc34a3d704d245385c71942c1732db9fee0b1/.agent/docs/architecture/supported-workflows.md), and [`configuration-list.md`](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/blob/8c3fc34a3d704d245385c71942c1732db9fee0b1/.agent/docs/customization/configuration-list.md). |
| coding_workflow / generic | Read docs and linked context first | pass | 6/6 | PR scope matches linked issue [#142](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/issues/142) and parent plan [#126](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/issues/126): config/docs boundary only, no Project creation, no variable mutation, no Project field sync. |
| coding_workflow / generic | Make surgical changes | pass | 8/8 | The diff is limited to the project-manager workflow, prompt, focused docs, and an envelope test; it does not alter unrelated routes or broader automation. |
| coding_workflow / generic | Reuse existing code | pass | 8/8 | Threads the Project target through the existing `agent-project-manager.yml` and `project-manager.md` surfaces instead of adding a new route or rebuilding project-management machinery. |
| coding_workflow / generic | No per-file inventory tables in docs | pass | 6/6 | Documentation describes behavior and boundaries conceptually; it does not add stale per-file inventory tables. |
| coding_style / generic | Prefer simple implementations | pass | 7/7 | Implements the staged request with lightweight input resolution, prompt context, docs, and envelope assertions, while deferring setup route, Project creation, and field sync as requested. |

## Notes

- The strongest score drivers are that the PR stays inside the child issue’s intended boundary and keeps Project-backed management explicitly experimental/context-only.
- The implementation also preserves the prior legacy/fallback label behavior from child issue `#140` while adding a Project target contract.
- I did not rerun the PR’s tests; this review is scored from the live PR metadata, diff, linked issues, and stated verification.

## Findings

- **INFO:** No rubric-specific follow-up needed.

## Final Rubric Verdict

PASS

## AI Review Synthesis

<!-- sepo-agent-review-synthesis -->

> Dual-agent review by **Claude** and **Codex**.
> Requested by @lolipopshock.

## Summary of PR/Issue
PR #143 implements child issue #142 under parent plan #126. It adds experimental GitHub Project target configuration for the project-manager workflow via Project ID/URL inputs and repo variables. The PR threads the resolved target into the project-manager prompt as context only, while preserving no-Project summary/dry-run behavior. It also updates docs and envelope coverage to keep Project-backed planning clearly separated from legacy/fallback priority and effort label writes.

## Review
Both independent reviewers found the PR scoped correctly and did not identify blocking or required branch-change issues. One reviewer noted a few non-blocking implementation details, but they were informational and consistent with existing workflow patterns.

| Issue | Severity | Description |
| -- | -- | -- |
| No actionable issues found | INFO | The PR matches #142 and the staged #126 plan: Project target config is context-only, docs/tests are updated, and existing dry-run/legacy label behavior is preserved. |

## Progress
- Existing PR discussion includes an implementation summary and a rubrics review marked PASS.
- Independent reviews agree there are no blocking issues.
- No inline comments were posted because there were no unresolved, concrete, line-specific issues requiring direct reviewer feedback.
- Non-blocking notes about empty manual inputs inheriting repo vars, loose Project ID shape validation, and currently unused raw outputs are informational.

## Recommended Next Step
NO_AUTOMATED_ACTION: no unresolved actionable branch changes remain for an automated fix-pr pass.

## Final Verdict
SHIP

## Action Items
- [ ] No required branch-change action items.

title: Add Project target config for project management
